steps.title: Separate the two sentences
steps.content: The first clause says the black sand dazzles visitors. The next idea begins with When they take a bit of sand home and has its own main clause, so the first sentence should end before that dependent clause begins.

steps.title: Avoid a run-on
steps.content: Choice A gives no punctuation between the ideas. Choice B uses only a comma, creating a comma splice. Choice D creates an awkward and when construction. A period correctly ends the first sentence and begins the next. The answer is C.
